# Wave H6 — Cross-team dedup, data-driven anomaly threshold, dev safety

## Lessons baked in
- Kill running servers before any DB mutation script (caused "database disk image is malformed" in dev today). Lane 3 codifies this.

## Lanes

| Lane | Fleet | Effort | Scope | Blocked by |
|------|-------|--------|-------|------------|
| 1 | Han 😉🚀 | M | Cross-team player dedup script + apply for known dups | — |
| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | S | Replace `goals > 12` heuristic with data-driven 3σ threshold | — |
| 3 | Leia 👑💁‍♀️ | S | Pre-migration safety check + dev-loop docs | — |

## Lane scope contracts (locked)

### Lane 1 (Han) — Cross-team player dedup
- NEW `packages/ingest/src/scripts/dedupCrossTeam.ts` — finds players with the same exact name on multiple teams; for each pair prints a proposed merge plan with stat-row totals on each side; `--apply` to commit.
- Default keep-side rule: the player_id with the most games_played (tie-break: lower id).
- Repoint `player_stats.player_id`; delete the loser row only when no game_id collisions occur. Otherwise log warning.
- Add `dedup:cross-team` npm script.
- Apply to the 4 known dups discovered in H5 (Mason Proctor #49718/#75525, Javier Gonzalez-Cruz #49724/#78746). After --apply, run integrity check.
- Tests in `__tests__/dedupCrossTeam.test.ts` (in-memory DB).

### Lane 2 (Yoda) — Data-driven anomaly threshold
- EDIT `packages/web/src/views/playerDetail.ts` — replace hardcoded `goals > 12` heuristic.
- Compute mean+stdev of goals per game across season. Mark a row anomalous if `goals > mean + 3*stdev`.
- Stats can be derived inline from the same per-game array already on the page (`stats: PlayerPerGameStat[]`).
- Add helper `packages/web/src/util/zscore.ts` with `mean`, `stdev`, `isOutlier(value, all)`.
- Test coverage: cases for typical row, extreme row, single-row season (no anomaly possible).

### Lane 3 (Leia) — Pre-migration safety
- NEW `packages/ingest/src/scripts/lib/checkServerProcs.ts` — detects running `pll/server` processes via `pgrep -lf` and warns with PID + start time.
- WIRE into `splitCompositePlayers.ts`, `dedupCrossTeam.ts` (Han's new file), `dedupPlayers.ts`, `dedupTeams.ts`. Each `--apply` path calls the check first; if servers found and `--force` not passed, exit 1 with friendly message.
- Update `.github/copilot-instructions.md` + machine copy at `~/.github/copilot-instructions.md` — short paragraph: "Before any `--apply` migration: stop dev servers, then run."
- Tests for the helper.

| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | ✅ Replaced `goals > 12` with `isOutlier` (3σ + floor=8). New `util/zscore.ts` + 6 tests. typecheck + 34 web tests green. |

Manual verify: `--apply` refuses with friendly multi-line message + PID + start time when a fake server runs; `--apply --force` proceeds. Updated both copilot-instructions files (repo + `~/.github/`). Han's `dedupCrossTeam.ts` was untracked at commit time → next agent should add `if (apply) checkServerProcs({ force: argv.includes('--force') });` near top of its `main()`. |
